summary: Markets remain tethered to the Iran conflict and the trajectory of oil prices, with reports that Trump told aides he is willing to end the U.S. military campaign against Iran even if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed sending futures higher but leaving deep uncertainty intact.
tweet: Iran de-escalation signals lifted futures but oil above $100 and a Fed on hold keep fear entrenched — the Strait of Hormuz is still the market's pressure valve.
